source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
source_quote_or_context: If you don't feel confident in the direction that your team is moving, the right answer is to micromanage, but do it in a very tactical and a very temporary way.
core_belief: Hands-off leadership when direction is wrong is worse than temporary tactical control.
operational_pattern: Map confidence×autonomy; use scalable leadership when aligned; selective micromanagement until frameworks transfer decision rights.
organizational_archetype: Product leadership coach for CPOs with builder instincts
hidden_assumptions: Senior leaders should only do strategy
contradictions: Praises famous micromanagers while coaching against the label
operational_adaptations: Exit when team exceeds leader on decisions
ai_era_implications: AI leaders must intervene when teams ship autonomy theater without eval discipline

source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
source_quote_or_context: The advantage a smaller company has really is in latency... from an idea to actually being able to test that idea.
core_belief: Speed is cycle time to learning, not output volume.
operational_pattern: Shrink work batches; ship testable slices daily; measure idea-to-result latency.
organizational_archetype: Founder after big-co career (Outpace, ex-Tinder CPO)
behavioral_signals: Founders say startup feels slower at first
operational_adaptations: Conviction decisions when N too small
ai_era_implications: AI startups win on trace/eval iteration speed

source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
source_quote_or_context: The company mission, the company strategy, the product strategy, the product roadmap, and the product goals all work together as a system.
core_belief: Prioritization fights mean missing strategy.
operational_pattern: Debug bottom-up; define top-down; use Tinder vs Hinge to decide copy vs diverge.
organizational_archetype: VP Product with PMs stuck between equal options
hidden_assumptions: Vision must be separate from mission
contradictions: Goals after roadmap but strategy before goals
ai_era_implications: AI bets need capability strategy before autonomy metrics

source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
source_quote_or_context: If you don't understand how to move a particular metric, then the right goal is to set a goal to increase your understanding.
core_belief: Outcome obsession ≠ committing to unmoved metrics.
operational_pattern: Classify goals as understanding, dependency, execution, or strategic risk; use hit×know-why 2×2.
ai_era_implications: Agent teams need failure-taxonomy goals before resolution-rate targets

source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
source_quote_or_context: The strategy doc wouldn't be complete without wireframes... everyone takes away a different interpretation when you talk about strategy in words alone.
core_belief: Strategy must bind pixel-level tradeoffs or it is not strategy.
operational_pattern: Require low-fi wireframes with strategy reviews; nav-bar litmus for mobile.
ai_era_implications: AI prototypes start with schemas/specs before UI polish

source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
source_quote_or_context: At a startup, you can't do that [experimental approach]... shift to much more of a conviction-oriented approach.
core_belief: Pre-scale decisions are conviction bets tested by speed.
operational_pattern: Set informed-conviction threshold; ship; pivot on qualitative signal.
ai_era_implications: Pre-PMF AI needs trace conviction loops not benchmarks

source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
source_quote_or_context: If you're building APIs, then your experience is actually the API spec.
core_belief: PM craft defines experience artifacts, not feature lists.
operational_pattern: 12-competency model: UX design = experience for APIs, models, or UI.
contradictions: Episode title: JSON before design for AI prototypes
ai_era_implications: AI PMs own prompt/schema/eval as primary UX

source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
source_quote_or_context: Not AI as a replacement for people, but AI as a way to amplify people.
core_belief: Human expertise stays in the loop; AI multiplies judgment throughput.
operational_pattern: Participant text→AI draft feedback→coach edit; style-tuned prompts.
contradictions: Accelerated AI roadmap after delay plan
ai_era_implications: Enterprise template: expert amplification with structured artifacts

source_episode: Why AI evals are the hottest new skill for product builders | Hamel Husain & Shreya Shankar
source_quote_or_context: The first step in conquering data like this is just to write notes... called error analysis.
core_belief: You cannot test what you have not observed in traces.
operational_pattern: Sample traces; product-hat notes; defer taxonomy until open-coding saturation.
behavioral_signals: Buy eval SaaS without reading traces
ai_era_implications: Default quality gate for customer-facing agents

source_episode: Why AI evals are the hottest new skill for product builders | Hamel Husain & Shreya Shankar
source_quote_or_context: You can appoint one person whose taste that you trust... don't make this process so expensive that you can't do it.
core_belief: Eval discovery must stay cheap enough to run continuously.
operational_pattern: Single domain owner labels traces; avoid committee in open coding.
operational_adaptations: Charter one dictator per failure domain
ai_era_implications: PM ownership of agent taste is explicit

source_episode: Why AI evals are the hottest new skill for product builders | Hamel Husain & Shreya Shankar
source_quote_or_context: This is the user experience of your product... the product person should be super involved.
core_belief: Agent conversations are the product; technical correctness ≠ success.
operational_pattern: Note 'should have handed off to human' when lead-management requires it.
hidden_assumptions: Engineers infer product intent from logs
contradictions: Real system prompt shown in teaching
ai_era_implications: PM eval literacy as core as funnel analysis

source_episode: Why AI evals are the hottest new skill for product builders | Hamel Husain & Shreya Shankar
source_quote_or_context: Evals is a big spectrum... unit tests are a very small part of that very big puzzle.
core_belief: One measurement layer cannot cover stochastic LLM behavior.
operational_pattern: Combine trace analytics, cohort metrics, thumbs, targeted automated checks.
organizational_archetype: Teams treating evals as CI only
behavioral_signals: Cite LM Arena not customer data
ai_era_implications: VoC-style continuous trace science

source_episode: Why AI evals are the hottest new skill for product builders | Hamel Husain & Shreya Shankar
source_quote_or_context: The goal is not to do evals perfectly, it's to actionably improve your product.
core_belief: Eval ROI is product delta, not measurement elegance.
operational_pattern: Minimum viable eval loop tied to fix backlog.
organizational_archetype: Teams burned by bad eval programs
operational_adaptations: Every metric tied to owner and ship threshold
ai_era_implications: Anti-pattern: eval teams detached from roadmap

source_episode: Why AI evals are the hottest new skill for product builders | Hamel Husain & Shreya Shankar
source_quote_or_context: You shouldn't have tried to create your taxonomy of failures as you're open coding.
core_belief: Taxonomy emerges from data; premature categories blind you.
operational_pattern: Open codes freely → cluster axial codes → prioritize → build judges per cluster.
pressure_scenarios: Leadership wants eval deck before data
contradictions: Package LLM methods not invent social science
ai_era_implications: Standard agent QA maturity model

source_episode: Why AI evals are the hottest new skill for product builders | Hamel Husain & Shreya Shankar
source_quote_or_context: When it goes to building an LLM as a judge, you need a binary score.
core_belief: Operational evals need fast decisive automated checks.
operational_pattern: Binary judge or code check per failure mode; avoid Likert in CI.
contradictions: Human rubrics vs binary ops evals
operational_adaptations: Code checks first; binary LLM second
ai_era_implications: Ops eval stack ≠ training rubric stack

pattern_name: Ground A/B Tests in Error Analysis
source_episode: Why AI evals are the hottest new skill for product builders | Hamel Husain & Shreya Shankar
source_quote_or_context: A lot of people prematurely do A/B tests, because they've never done any error analysis in the first place.
core_belief: Experimentation without observation optimizes imaginary problems.
operational_pattern: Block A/B until failure modes validated on traces.
contradictions: Teach experimentation while restricting premature tests
operational_adaptations: Trace evidence required in experiment briefs
ai_era_implications: Agent A/B needs failure-mode regression guards

source_episode: Why experts writing AI evals is creating the fastest-growing companies in history | Brendan Foody
source_quote_or_context: If the model is the product, then the eval is the product requirement document.
core_belief: Capability specification is the bottleneck to model economic value.
operational_pattern: Experts write rubrics; labs iterate experiments; same artifact for sales proof.
organizational_archetype: Mercor supplying experts to top labs
operational_adaptations: Map workflows to eval PRDs before agents
ai_era_implications: Every AI product team runs hidden expert rubrics

source_episode: Why experts writing AI evals is creating the fastest-growing companies in history | Brendan Foody
source_quote_or_context: Find the customer that's surprisingly easy to sell into.
core_belief: Hard marginal sales signal wrong wedge.
operational_pattern: Stay stubborn on thesis, flexible on form; abandon hard-sell segments.
organizational_archetype: Mercor pivot from hiring automation to expert eval labor
hidden_assumptions: Persistence on wrong product is virtue
ai_era_implications: Find lab/enterprise pull pockets not incremental SaaS

source_episode: Why experts writing AI evals is creating the fastest-growing companies in history | Brendan Foody
source_quote_or_context: Optimize everything around leading indicators of those markets.
core_belief: Why-now must be market-structure change not feature automation.
operational_pattern: Align to frontier benchmarks wealthy buyers fund.
emotional_political_dynamics: Board wants SaaS metrics on lab business
ai_era_implications: Strategy = moving benchmark target

source_episode: Why experts writing AI evals is creating the fastest-growing companies in history | Brendan Foody
source_quote_or_context: Can-do attitude... really high standards... intensity.
core_belief: Legendary outcomes need absurd goals plus elite talent plus ownership.
operational_pattern: Public insane targets; patient first 10 hires; accelerate post demand vacuum.
contradictions: Denies mandating 6-9-9 while describing midnight oil
behavioral_signals: Benchmark 50M miss then hit in 2 weeks
operational_adaptations: Ambitious targets; ops hire at inflection
ai_era_implications: AI winners combine lab-intensity with expert-marketplace ops

source_episode: Why experts writing AI evals is creating the fastest-growing companies in history | Brendan Foody
source_quote_or_context: If we make everyone 10 times more productive, that'll increase demand, not reduce it.
core_belief: AI expands output in elastic markets.
operational_pattern: Bet on software, PM, consulting; AI as Iron Man suit in interviews.
ai_era_implications: PM/engineering remain elastic

source_episode: Why experts writing AI evals is creating the fastest-growing companies in history | Brendan Foody
source_quote_or_context: The generalists are no longer needed. What they really need is experts.
core_belief: Post-training gains need scarce human knowledge and failure discovery.
operational_pattern: PhDs break models; step-by-step data; top 10% labelers drive lift.
ai_era_implications: Every company fine-tune IP = expert loops

source_episode: Why experts writing AI evals is creating the fastest-growing companies in history | Brendan Foody
source_quote_or_context: For the last year and a half... we've had no one in sales and marketing.
core_belief: Six-star experience beats early marketing sophistication.
operational_pattern: 100% product until pull undeniable; PMs only recently on GTM.
operational_adaptations: Delay marketing until flagship expansion unprompted
ai_era_implications: In expert-data markets product quality is distribution

source_episode: Why experts writing AI evals is creating the fastest-growing companies in history | Brendan Foody
source_quote_or_context: Especially for our first 10 people, we were just so patient and disciplined.
core_belief: Talent density at foundation beats headcount; speed follows vacuum proof.
operational_pattern: First ~10: extreme bar; 10→100: accept variance, prioritize ramp.
hidden_assumptions: Always hire slow or always fast
ai_era_implications: First 10 include eval practitioners + lab relationships

source_episode: The ultimate guide to AEO: How to get ChatGPT to recommend your product | Ethan Smith
source_quote_or_context: You need to get mentioned as many times as possible.
core_belief: Answer engines reward distributed mentions not single #1 link.
operational_pattern: Head queries: Reddit, YouTube, blogs, affiliates + SEO landing pages.
ai_era_implications: Discovery shifts to multi-surface mentions

source_episode: The ultimate guide to AEO: How to get ChatGPT to recommend your product | Ethan Smith
source_quote_or_context: You can get mentioned by a citation tomorrow and start showing up immediately.
core_belief: Citations remove domain-authority time penalty.
operational_pattern: Launch PR, Reddit, YouTube for instant LLM visibility.
hidden_assumptions: Wait until Series B for search
ai_era_implications: Startups compete on mention velocity

source_episode: The ultimate guide to AEO: How to get ChatGPT to recommend your product | Ethan Smith
source_quote_or_context: Webflow saw a 6X conversion rate difference between LLM traffic and Google Search traffic.
core_belief: Answer-engine visitors arrive with richer summarized context.
operational_pattern: Instrument LLM referrers; justify AEO on conversion not sessions.
ai_era_implications: LLM-assisted intent pipeline stage

source_episode: The ultimate guide to AEO: How to get ChatGPT to recommend your product | Ethan Smith
source_quote_or_context: The tail is just much, much larger... around 25 words versus Google... around six.
core_belief: Chat queries are multi-step conversations.
operational_pattern: Content depth for follow-up prompts; map conversation branches.
contradictions: Head and tail both differ from Google
ai_era_implications: Agents generate 25+ word task chains

source_episode: The ultimate guide to AEO: How to get ChatGPT to recommend your product | Ethan Smith
source_quote_or_context: Anything can be optimized... Please don't spam my product.
core_belief: AEO is rule discovery; platforms punish abuse.
operational_pattern: Learn preferred citations; avoid synthetic spam; expect algo updates.
hidden_assumptions: Nick Turley means no AEO work
ai_era_implications: Cat-mouse moves to LLM answer layers

source_episode: The ultimate guide to AEO: How to get ChatGPT to recommend your product | Ethan Smith
source_quote_or_context: Everything that works in SEO works in AEO, but there are additional things beyond SEO.
core_belief: Extend SEO don't replace it.
operational_pattern: High-volume landing pages plus YouTube/Reddit mention programs.
behavioral_signals: Rename SEO to AEO without skills
ai_era_implications: Google slice stable, pie grows—multi-engine norm

source_episode: The ultimate guide to AEO: How to get ChatGPT to recommend your product | Ethan Smith
source_quote_or_context: The clickability of the answer is increased... around January.
core_belief: Platform UX changes activate latent demand.
operational_pattern: Track OpenAI/Google UI changes as leading indicators; fix attribution pre-spike.
ai_era_implications: Answer UI becomes commerce surface

source_episode: The ultimate guide to AEO: How to get ChatGPT to recommend your product | Ethan Smith
source_quote_or_context: Google's slice of the pie stays the same. The pie gets bigger.
core_belief: Augment channels don't panic-abandon SEO.
operational_pattern: Invest in Google AI Overviews and ChatGPT citations.
ai_era_implications: Discovery oligopoly diversifies

pattern_name: Run Toward Fear on Dual-Bad Choices
source_episode: $46B of hard truths from Ben Horowitz: Why founders fail and why you need to run toward fear
source_quote_or_context: The thing that causes you to hesitate is both decisions are horrible.
core_belief: CEO value is choosing the less-catastrophic path.
operational_pattern: Name both horrors; decide fast (IPO at $2M TTM vs bankruptcy).
contradictions: Can't coach courage—only point out hesitation
ai_era_implications: AI pivots need public insane bets

source_episode: $46B of hard truths from Ben Horowitz: Why founders fail and why you need to run toward fear
source_quote_or_context: If everybody agrees with the decision, then you didn't add any value.
core_belief: CEO rent is paid for disagreeable necessary calls.
operational_pattern: Seek input; own contrarian call; accept firing if wrong.
pressure_scenarios: Board wants collaborative CEO on layoffs
ai_era_implications: CEO must say no to committee chatbot plans

source_episode: $46B of hard truths from Ben Horowitz: Why founders fail and why you need to run toward fear
source_quote_or_context: You don't make people great. You find people that make you great.
core_belief: CEO scales via world-class functional leaders not C-suite remediation.
operational_pattern: Replace non-leveraging execs; VP Eng develops engineers, CEO doesn't create CFOs.
failure_modes: CEO as remedial trainer for months
ai_era_implications: Don't train CMO on marketing from zero

source_episode: $46B of hard truths from Ben Horowitz: Why founders fail and why you need to run toward fear
source_quote_or_context: The only reason to start a company is because you have an irrational desire... it's not worth the money.
core_belief: Financial motive insufficient for CEO suffering.
operational_pattern: Screen for mission obsession; Citigroup CEO: $1.6B wasn't worth it.
hidden_assumptions: Rational ROI works for joining Meta
operational_adaptations: Mission stress test in partner meetings
ai_era_implications: AI gold rush attracts mercenaries—filter mission

source_episode: $46B of hard truths from Ben Horowitz: Why founders fail and why you need to run toward fear
source_quote_or_context: All plane crashes are a series of bad decisions.
core_belief: Break sunk-cost early; small hard fixes compound.
operational_pattern: JFK Jr. parable; Shaka belief revision in solitary.
ai_era_implications: Kill sunk-cost non-AI product lines earlier

source_episode: $46B of hard truths from Ben Horowitz: Why founders fail and why you need to run toward fear
source_quote_or_context: Very small compared to what I did to myself.
core_belief: Believing external narrative destroys CEOs.
operational_pattern: Separate feedback from identity; Shaka post-solitary model.
ai_era_implications: AI disruption anxiety self-fulfilling if internalized

source_episode: $46B of hard truths from Ben Horowitz: Why founders fail and why you need to run toward fear
source_quote_or_context: The job is fundamentally a leadership job... that's exactly what the PM is.
core_belief: PM success = product works via influence not authority.
operational_pattern: Train leadership not task lists; Good PM/Bad PM leadership core.
ai_era_implications: AI PMs lead eval outcomes without reports

source_episode: $46B of hard truths from Ben Horowitz: Why founders fail and why you need to run toward fear
source_quote_or_context: I'll write you a check for $10 million because this company, you need to build a company.
core_belief: Academic founders underestimate scope.
operational_pattern: Databricks: $10M not $200K; find internal CEO (Ali) not campus win.
organizational_archetype: Ben vs six professors + Jan Stoicka
operational_adaptations: Default round size to ambition test
ai_era_implications: Professors pitch labeling lifestyle businesses

source_episode: How Devin replaces your junior engineers with infinite AI interns that never sleep | Scott Wu
source_quote_or_context: I really like the phrase jagged intelligence.
core_belief: Agents are uneven juniors—scope to strengths.
operational_pattern: Map task types to capability profile; human-close weak classes.
hidden_assumptions: Agent equals senior if benchmark high
behavioral_signals: Rage at single failure after 90% win
ai_era_implications: All agents have jagged profiles

source_episode: How Devin replaces your junior engineers with infinite AI interns that never sleep | Scott Wu
source_quote_or_context: Most folks on the team are definitely working with up to five Devins at once.
core_belief: Throughput scales via parallel async agents.
operational_pattern: Queue five daily tasks; intervene on plan/UX only.
ai_era_implications: 1 engineer : N concurrent agents

source_episode: How Devin replaces your junior engineers with infinite AI interns that never sleep | Scott Wu
source_quote_or_context: Allowing engineers to go from bricklayer to architect.
core_belief: Value moves to problem definition and architecture.
operational_pattern: Humans own spec/architecture/complex 10–20%; agents execute bulk.
ai_era_implications: PMs and eng both architects

source_episode: How Devin replaces your junior engineers with infinite AI interns that never sleep | Scott Wu
source_quote_or_context: Let's work on getting the repository set up first... couple one pointer tasks.
core_belief: Agent onboarding mirrors human junior onboarding.
operational_pattern: VM+CI setup; 1-pointer tasks; ban full rearchitecture until familiar.
hidden_assumptions: Agent reads entire repo day one
ai_era_implications: Enterprise agent rollouts need onboarding SOP

source_episode: How Devin replaces your junior engineers with infinite AI interns that never sleep | Scott Wu
source_quote_or_context: A lot of the features we've built... figuring out how to make working with Devin as smooth as possible.
core_belief: Capabilities and collaboration UX co-determine value.
operational_pattern: Slack/Linear/GitHub integrations, planning, touch-up; measure merge rate not IQ.
ai_era_implications: Winning AI eng products are workflow companies

source_episode: How Devin replaces your junior engineers with infinite AI interns that never sleep | Scott Wu
source_quote_or_context: The best workflow... multiple Devins... run them asynchronously.
core_belief: Synchronous pairing underuses agent leverage.
operational_pattern: Kickoff → agent runs → review at end; parallel agents.
hidden_assumptions: Watch agent type in real time always
contradictions: Sync demo impresses but doesn't scale
ai_era_implications: Future eng days are async orchestration

pattern_name: RL and Agent Bet from Founding
source_episode: How Devin replaces your junior engineers with infinite AI interns that never sleep | Scott Wu
source_quote_or_context: Reinforcement learning was really working... shift from text completion to agents.
core_belief: Next paradigm is RL + autonomous tool loops.
operational_pattern: Bet coding for automated eval; agent surface early; expect pivots.
operational_adaptations: Pivot budget; grounding question on future of SWE
ai_era_implications: Coding cos reconcile RL gains with UX

source_episode: How Devin replaces your junior engineers with infinite AI interns that never sleep | Scott Wu
source_quote_or_context: There's going to be way more programmers and way more engineers a few years from now.
core_belief: Lower build cost increases demand for builders.
operational_pattern: Hire humans for architecture/review; agents expand output.
operational_adaptations: Grow eng with higher agent:human ratio
ai_era_implications: Software construction glut increases builder demand

source_episode: How we restructured Airtable's entire org for AI | Howie Liu
source_quote_or_context: Every software product... has to be refounded.
core_belief: Legacy may be net-negative vs greenfield.
operational_pattern: Found-from-scratch exercise; compare AI-native vs legacy leverage.
hidden_assumptions: Always iterate; never throw away distribution
ai_era_implications: Incumbents need refound decision gate

source_episode: How we restructured Airtable's entire org for AI | Howie Liu
source_quote_or_context: We have the fast thinking group... ship capabilities on a near weekly basis... slow thinking group.
core_belief: AI velocity and enterprise depth need separate modes.
operational_pattern: AI platform ships weekly jaw-drops; slow ships HyperDB-scale infra; fast=funnel, slow=expansion.
ai_era_implications: SaaS incumbent bimodal EPD template

source_episode: How we restructured Airtable's entire org for AI | Howie Liu
source_quote_or_context: You have to be of the details... chief taste maker.
core_belief: CEO must build/play with AI primitives to set taste.
operational_pattern: Cut standing 1:1s; topical alpha meetings; highest internal inference spend.
contradictions: Dan Shipper CEO daily ChatGPT signal
operational_adaptations: CEO inference budget as cultural KPI
ai_era_implications: IC CEO required for AI transformation

source_episode: How we restructured Airtable's entire org for AI | Howie Liu
source_quote_or_context: Hundreds of dollars spent on this exercise is trivial compared to the potential strategic value.
core_belief: Greedy smart compute beats false frugality.
operational_pattern: Map-reduce LLM over sales transcripts for positioning insights.
organizational_archetype: Airtable LLM map-reduce on sales calls
contradictions: Could be tweeted as irresponsible COGS
ai_era_implications: Exec AI budget for strategic inference

source_episode: How we restructured Airtable's entire org for AI | Howie Liu
source_quote_or_context: The advent of the polymath... full stack builders are having their renaissance.
core_belief: 500 touchpoints can't survive 500 new tools/week.
operational_pattern: PM/design/eng overlap; collapse handoffs on AI initiatives.
ai_era_implications: Job descriptions merge PM+proto+eval

source_episode: How we restructured Airtable's entire org for AI | Howie Liu
source_quote_or_context: Cut my one-on-one roster... meetings very urgency driven.
core_belief: Standing rituals block CEO building time.
operational_pattern: Topical alpha meetings; in-person lunch monthly not weekly 1:1 grid.
contradictions: Still has AI sprint weekly check-in

source_episode: How we restructured Airtable's entire org for AI | Howie Liu
source_quote_or_context: Cancel all your meetings for... a week and just go play around with every AI product.
core_belief: Fluency requires unstructured exploration.
operational_pattern: Mandate AI play weeks before roadmap commitments.
ai_era_implications: Org AI fluency prerequisite

source_episode: How we restructured Airtable's entire org for AI | Howie Liu
source_quote_or_context: Evals are more useful once you've converged on the basic scaffold of the form factor.
core_belief: Premature eval rigor on shifting UX wastes effort.
operational_pattern: Prototype creatively → converged use cases → eval harness.
operational_adaptations: Phase-gate: exploration OKR then eval OKR
ai_era_implications: Mature agents need evals; discovery tolerates vibes

source_episode: How 80,000 companies build with AI: products as organisms, the death of org charts, and why agents will outnumber employees by 2026 | Asha Sharma
source_quote_or_context: Living organisms that just get better with the more interactions... metabolism of a product team.
core_belief: Ship model-forward products with continuous post-training loops.
operational_pattern: Instrument interactions; rewards design; A/B post-train; parallel fine-tune tracks.
operational_adaptations: Metabolism KPI: signal to weight update

source_episode: How 80,000 companies build with AI: products as organisms, the death of org charts, and why agents will outnumber employees by 2026 | Asha Sharma
source_quote_or_context: It's all about the loop, not the lane.
core_belief: Owning the improve loop beats functional title.
operational_pattern: Break 500-touchpoint launches; full-stack builders; observability culture.
ai_era_implications: Org charts dissolve into work charts

source_episode: How 80,000 companies build with AI: products as organisms, the death of org charts, and why agents will outnumber employees by 2026 | Asha Sharma
source_quote_or_context: The org chart starts to become the work chart.
core_belief: Tasks route to agents+humans by capability not hierarchy.
operational_pattern: Missions as prompts/goals for agent teams; humans set objectives and review.
operational_adaptations: Pilot agent teams on conversion KPIs
ai_era_implications: Future org is task graph not tree

source_episode: How 80,000 companies build with AI: products as organisms, the death of org charts, and why agents will outnumber employees by 2026 | Asha Sharma
source_quote_or_context: We think about it as what season are we in?
core_belief: Annual roadmaps invalid—plan in capability seasons.
operational_pattern: Tag initiatives by season; replan on GPT-class shifts.
ai_era_implications: Planning must be season-based

source_episode: How 80,000 companies build with AI: products as organisms, the death of org charts, and why agents will outnumber employees by 2026 | Asha Sharma
source_quote_or_context: Stream of text just connects better with LLMs... composability and not the canvas.
core_belief: Design for agent consumption first; GUI second.
operational_pattern: API/Terraform mindset; artifacts agents can read.
ai_era_implications: GUI optional projection of code-native core

source_episode: How 80,000 companies build with AI: products as organisms, the death of org charts, and why agents will outnumber employees by 2026 | Asha Sharma
source_quote_or_context: Everybody becomes AI fluent → apply AI to existing process → use it to inflect growth.
core_belief: Skip steps → AI-for-AI's-sake without P&L.
operational_pattern: Stage 1 fluency; stage 2 process improvement; stage 3 growth/agents.
ai_era_implications: Enterprise three-step ladder standard

source_episode: How 80,000 companies build with AI: products as organisms, the death of org charts, and why agents will outnumber employees by 2026 | Asha Sharma
source_quote_or_context: Build for the slope instead of the snapshot of where you are.
core_belief: Platform bets must swap components as tech churns.
operational_pattern: App-server layer; modular stack; quarterly tech review.
ai_era_implications: Moat is learning slope not snapshot features

source_episode: How 80,000 companies build with AI: products as organisms, the death of org charts, and why agents will outnumber employees by 2026 | Asha Sharma
source_quote_or_context: Once a model hits 30 billion parameters... optimize on the loop.
core_belief: Pre-train CapEx uneconomic for most; proprietary loops are moat.
operational_pattern: Expert annotation; synthetic data; rigorous A/B; parallel tracks.
contradictions: Model cos and SaaS both post-train
operational_adaptations: Post-train team + data rights day one
ai_era_implications: Every product co becomes post-training co

source_episode: Inside the expert network training every frontier AI model | Garrett Lord
source_quote_or_context: The only moat in human data is access to an audience.
core_belief: Expert distribution beats commodity labeling ops.
operational_pattern: Leverage Handshake 18M professionals, 500k PhDs, zero-CAC supply.
organizational_archetype: Handshake graph; AI spinout 0→50M ARR in 4 months
ai_era_implications: Audience platforms pivot to expert eval supply

source_episode: Inside the expert network training every frontier AI model | Garrett Lord
source_quote_or_context: The generalists are no longer needed. What they really need is experts.
core_belief: Post-training needs domain expertise at model failure frontier.
operational_pattern: PhDs break models; document steps; trajectories with tool narration.
operational_adaptations: Match expert tier to capability gap
ai_era_implications: Human data = expert failure hunting

source_episode: Inside the expert network training every frontier AI model | Garrett Lord
source_quote_or_context: If you are a PhD in physics, you can go in multiple subdomains.
core_belief: Training data from proven failures + correct step-by-step truth.
operational_pattern: Probe non-deterministic errors; capture steps 6–10 failures.
ai_era_implications: Eval rubrics from break-and-fix loops

source_episode: Inside the expert network training every frontier AI model | Garrett Lord
source_quote_or_context: They care about quality first... volume... speed.
core_belief: Labs run parallel hypotheses—suppliers turn in days at scale.
operational_pattern: Unit-level gain on own GPUs; kill pipelines that don't move benchmarks.
organizational_archetype: Handshake rents GPUs; internal post-training team
operational_adaptations: Dashboard: quality, volume, TAT per hypothesis
ai_era_implications: AI data supply chain manufacturing KPIs

source_episode: Inside the expert network training every frontier AI model | Garrett Lord
source_quote_or_context: The top 10% of people will drive majority of the model improvement.
core_belief: Elite expert matching is competitive advantage.
operational_pattern: Proprietary fellow ranking; staff projects with top decile; 24h creative expert turnaround.
organizational_archetype: Harvard Lampoon, Emmy writers for creative evals
operational_adaptations: Invest in matcher ML + reputation graph
ai_era_implications: Talent marketplaces = top-decile matching wars

source_episode: Inside the expert network training every frontier AI model | Garrett Lord
source_quote_or_context: A trajectory is... your screen, your mouse... rubric models.
core_belief: Multimodal pro data + judge rubrics enable RL at scale.
operational_pattern: Record tool use with narration; JSON; rubrics when no single right answer.
ai_era_implications: Professional workflow data is next frontier

source_episode: Inside the expert network training every frontier AI model | Garrett Lord
source_quote_or_context: This is a business that you built on top of a business you've already had.
core_belief: Monetize latent graph when paradigm shifts.
operational_pattern: Jan 2025 AI launch; may exceed legacy revenue in <2 years.
hidden_assumptions: Protect core brand from AI side
operational_adaptations: Separate P&L, shared supply; move before outsider captures network
ai_era_implications: LinkedIn/Indeed playbook

source_episode: Inside the expert network training every frontier AI model | Garrett Lord
source_quote_or_context: There's unlimited demand... you have no regrets?
core_belief: Hyper-demand window rewards extreme execution.
operational_pattern: Fly to customers; late-night pushes; 6× data validation.
ai_era_implications: Historical AI infra gold rush ops tempo

source_episode: How Intercom rose from the ashes by betting everything on AI | Eoghan McCabe
source_quote_or_context: We were about to hit $0 net new ARR... had no choice.
core_belief: Existential stagnation enables harder pivots than comfortable incumbency.
operational_pattern: Return CEO; 6-week Fin prototype; ~$100M AI bet; accept legacy decline if AI works.
organizational_archetype: Intercom 14 years; declining net new ARR
hidden_assumptions: Thread needle sustaining old + dabbling AI
pressure_scenarios: Five quarters declining net new ARR
operational_adaptations: Use crisis honesty to justify all-in
ai_era_implications: SaaS plateau companies mimic no-choice framing

source_episode: How Intercom rose from the ashes by betting everything on AI | Eoghan McCabe
source_quote_or_context: Values designed to be a sharp knife to cut out the parts of the company.
core_belief: Values must selectively exclude not only inspire.
operational_pattern: Resilient, high standards, shareholder value; quarterly behavior+performance formula; exit below bar.
ai_era_implications: AI transformation needs cultural replacement

source_episode: How Intercom rose from the ashes by betting everything on AI | Eoghan McCabe
source_quote_or_context: We charge 99 cents to resolve tickets... revenue 100% aligned with the value.
core_belief: Simple outcome pricing heals seat-based complexity scars.
operational_pattern: Per resolution vs $20-30 human cost; margin from model cost decline.
organizational_archetype: Fin AI; early $1.20 cost vs $0.99 price
contradictions: Gave away $50M ARR simplifying legacy pricing
operational_adaptations: Model COGS roadmap before price commit
ai_era_implications: AI products standardize on outcome-based simple pricing

source_episode: How Intercom rose from the ashes by betting everything on AI | Eoghan McCabe
source_quote_or_context: There's also such a thing as employee, founder, product market fit.
core_belief: Transformation needs wartime people not peacetime.
operational_pattern: ~40% turnover; hire entrepreneurial resilient profile.
hidden_assumptions: Everyone can adapt with enough talk
behavioral_signals: Glassdoor worst CEO to high approval
ai_era_implications: Not every employee belongs in AI-first company

source_episode: How Intercom rose from the ashes by betting everything on AI | Eoghan McCabe
source_quote_or_context: CEO makes brave and hard decisions unilaterally.
core_belief: Founder mode beats committee for innovation bursts.
operational_pattern: Dictate service lane; ignore $80M side ARR; own board outcomes.
contradictions: Professional CEO deferral caused original drift
operational_adaptations: CEO owns outcomes including self-fire if wrong
ai_era_implications: AI pivot when committee stalls

source_episode: How Intercom rose from the ashes by betting everything on AI | Eoghan McCabe
source_quote_or_context: We're doing service... forget all the other stuff.
core_belief: Focus beats portfolio comfort vs dead Zendesk.
operational_pattern: Kill multi-product ambition; service-only; Fin disrupts seats.
ai_era_implications: Incumbents must starve legacy to feed agent

source_episode: How Intercom rose from the ashes by betting everything on AI | Eoghan McCabe
source_quote_or_context: An instantly available expert agent is so much more personal than waiting 2, 3, 4 days.
core_belief: AI can fulfill personal mission better than constrained human support orgs.
operational_pattern: Reconcile anti-bot history with Fin via speed, consistency, availability.
ai_era_implications: Support AI must pass more-personal-than-wait test

source_episode: How Intercom rose from the ashes by betting everything on AI | Eoghan McCabe
source_quote_or_context: We were only six weeks into the launch of GPT 3.5 when we actually had a beta version of Fin.
core_belief: Incumbent advantage is data + distribution if prototype cadence matches startups.
operational_pattern: 30k customers, billions support data points; ship beta Fin before market crowded.
operational_adaptations: Pre-built AI group; 6-week beta SLA for incumbents
ai_era_implications: Late SaaS winners ship Fin-class agents in weeks
